content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (post iter-030)

## HIGH — SNAP: next prover route is named & de-risked (do NOT re-route mul_assoc through the object pentagon)
- The keystone (`toPresheafLocalized_laxMonoidal`) + gate (`sectionsMul_eq_laxTensorator`) are LANDED
  axiom-clean. The graded `mul_assoc` (`hM` @L3336 / `sectionsMul_mul_assoc`) is now a SINGLE named
  construction, not a vague wall.
- **Assign the prover the faithful-functor reduction:** reduce `hM` (and the other `tensorPowAdd_*`
  coherences @3296/3318/3333/3420) to a *presheaf*-morphism identity via faithfulness of
  `toPresheafOfModules` (it is `Faithful`), then assemble from `LaxMonoidal.associativity
  (toPresheafLocalized X)` + the proven bridges (`tensorObjUnitIso_eq_localizedLeftUnitor`,
  `tensorBraiding_eq_localizedBraiding`, `sectionsMul_eq_laxTensorator`) + `μ_natural_left/right`. The
  point: everything then lives in ONE comp-instance (no `modulesLocalizedMonoidal ↔ X.Modules`
  dual-instance whnf bomb). This is the genuine "re-wire off the object pentagon".
- **Do NOT** assign a route that goes through `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localized` / `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs`
  (L2397, DO-NOT-TOUCH dual-instance wall) — the lax associativity law does NOT bypass the `tensorPowAdd`
  reindexing pentagon (confirmed this iter; the iter-028 reversal signal fired).
- Memory/PROGRESS correction REQUIRED: `W.IsMonoidal` / `LocalizedMonoidal` are NOT walls/unbuilt —
  `W_isMonoidal`, `modulesLocalizedMonoidal`, `tensorObjLocalizedIso` are present + axiom-clean in-file.

## HIGH — FBC: Route B did NOT close → reversal signal LIVE (re-scope the seed, NOT another fold/Route-A variant)
- `_concrete` reduced the crux to one clean `ModuleCat (R'⊗ₐB)` identity (geometric reindex = algebraic
  reassoc, Stacks 01I9 part (1)) but BOTH closing routes are dead: element (`Γ`-of-`pullbackComp`
  kernel-bomb) and mate (the dead fold). The crux frontier is now ~10 iters with 0 sorry-elimination.
- Per the iter-030 plan reversal signal + pc030 STUCK: **iter-031 = re-scope the FBC seed**, do NOT
  spend another prover budget on Route A (`vcomp_glue`/`catCommSq`) or any fold variant. Options for the
  planner: (a) effort-breaker on the residual module identity to split geometric=algebraic into smaller
  `ModuleCat` claims provable by `ext`+`TensorProduct` element calc that does NOT cross the X.Modules
  junction; (b) mathlib-analogist on "geometric reindex of iterated base-change pullback = algebraic
  reassociation" — is there a Mathlib `pushoutBaseChange`/`TensorProduct.assoc`-cocycle idiom; (c)
  accept the seed is too coarse and split the FBC goal upstream.
- If iter-031 chooses a prover lane, the residual module identity (`_concrete` @L2508) is the ONLY
  productive target — strictly better than the dead `mate_glue`. On close, retarget `_ring_square_natural`
  `exact` → `_concrete`.

## MEDIUM — code hygiene (lean-auditor iter030, report in logs/iter-030/)
- MAJOR-1: remove the 5 no-op `set_option maxHeartbeats N in` on `/-! -/` doc-comment headers in
  `FlatBaseChange.lean` (L2623, L2658–2661) — they are inert (set_option-in scopes the NEXT declaration,
  a doc-comment consumes it) and leave Seam-2/Seam-3 declarations on the module default. 4-iter repeat —
  bundle into the FBC cleanup/excision refactor iter; place the option directly on the decls that need it.
- MAJOR-2 + MINOR-5: `Picard/SectionGradedRingLocalized.lean` is ORPHANED (1306 lines, imported nowhere,
  outside the build graph; carries anachronistic iter-numbers + 6 dup-decls). Decide: delete, or re-import.
  Queue in the SNAP off-path-excision refactor.

## MEDIUM — blueprint realignment (carryover, lvbc fbc030 + snap030)
- FBC `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` (3 carryover majors from fbc029): Route-A `_catCommSq` prose still
  frames it as "realized" / as avoiding the mate apparatus it uses; `vcomp_glue` is uncovered; the b2-crux
  `mate_glue` proof prose is stale (body is sorry). Dispatch a blueprint-writer to realign — bundle with
  the FBC re-scope decision so the prose reflects the chosen iter-031 route.
- SNAP `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`: I corrected the `\lean{}` + added a `% NOTE` on the gate
  refinement. Planner still owes: (a) blueprint blocks for the 5 new decls; (b) an explicit gate sub-claim
  (`μ = sectionsMul ≫ Γ(tensorObjLocalizedIso)`) under `lem:sectionsFunctor_laxMonoidal`; (c) reconcile
  the "associativity needs no object pentagon" prose with the live Lean (`sectionsMul_mul_assoc` still
  routes through `tensorPowAdd_assoc`) — TRUE only once the faithful-functor reduction lands.

## Coverage debt — 96 unmatched (was 91; +5 new SNAP decls, all `leanok`)
Planner: author blueprint entries (file `Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean`):
- `localizedSheafification` (L1473) — strong-monoidal localized sheafification `L' = toMonoidalCategory`.
  Deps: `W_isMonoidal`, `localizedMonoidalUnitIso`, `Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory`.
- `toPresheafLocalized` (L1490) — `ι = toPresheafOfModules` out of the synonym.
- `localizedSheafificationAdjunction` (L1498) — `L' ⊣ ι` (= `sheafificationAdjunction` retyped).
- `toPresheafLocalized_laxMonoidal` (L1512) — KEYSTONE; maps to `lem:sectionsFunctor_laxMonoidal`.
  Deps: `Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`, decls 1–3.
- `sectionsMul_eq_laxTensorator` (L1718) — GATE; `μ_Γ = sectionsMul ≫ Γ(tensorObjLocalizedIso)`.
  Deps: `oplaxMonoidal_δ_eq`, `tensorObjLocalizedIso`, `Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal_μ`.
Remaining ~91 = off-path SNAP internals + FBC helpers (pre-existing; deferred to the SNAP off-path-excision
refactor as in prior iters).

## Do-NOT-retry (blocked)
- SNAP `mul_assoc` via the object pentagon / `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localized` (dual-instance whnf wall, L2397).
- FBC residual module identity via element route (`Γ`-of-`pullbackComp` kernel-bomb) or mate fold (dead ×3).
- FBC Route A `vcomp_glue`/`catCommSq` (EXHAUSTED, pc030 STUCK) and any `≪≫`-fold variant (dead ×3).
